title: Koth Melan
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Koth}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Bothanspy.jpg]] |name=Koth Melan |homeworld=[[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]][[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]]The events of the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]], which features Koth Melan's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]], are set in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |species=[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]][[Shadows of the Empire 3|''Shadows of the Empire'' 3]] |feathers= |eyes=Black{{SWGTCG|set=Agents of Deception|cardname=Koth Melan}} |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Clan Alya]] *[[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|War, like politics, sometimes makes for strange bedfellows. One uses the tools one has.|Koth Melan|}} '''Koth Melan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spymaster/Legends|spymaster]] and head of the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Death/Legends|executed]] his [[Koth Melan's father|father]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]] for supposed espionage, Melan was sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and sought to help them in order to regain his honor. Posing as an official of the [[Bothan Trade Mission]], Melan conducted operations throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including dealing with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] on the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. He preferred the role of spymaster, rarely venturing into the field and traveled with a cadre of bodyguards. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], his agents caught wind of a new Imperial military project that could pose a threat to the Alliance. He attempted to contact [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], but his message was instead intercepted by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and Dash Rendar. This proved to be a stroke of luck for Melan, since data pertinent to the project would be passing through the [[Both system]] on the [[MB-C1 medium transport|Mobquet medium transport]] ''[[Suprosa]]''. He enlisted [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Skywalker to train the Bothan [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of [[Blue Squadron (Bothan)|Blue Squadron]], and then, aboard Dash Rendar's ship, the [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']], participated in the [[Raid on the Suprosa|raid]] on the ''Suprosa''. With the data retrieved, Melan directed Skywalker and Rendar to [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]], where a team of Bothan slicers could decipher the data. While Melan's team were working on the retrieval, the Kothlis safehouse was attacked by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and in the ensuing gunfight, Melan was shot and killed. Despite Melan's death, the data was ferried to the Alliance by agents allied to [[Borsk Fey'lya]], and it was eventually discovered to be the plans for the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]]. ==Biography== ===Dishonor=== {{Quote|Twenty years ago the Empire executed my father for espionage.''"
"''That's one of the risks of this business, isn't it?''"
"''Yes, and one I take. But all Bothans are not spies, M. Rendar. My father was a teacher. He was guilty of nothing save trying to educate his students about the Empire.|Koth Melan and Dash Rendar|}} [[File:Koth_Melan.jpg|thumb|left|100px|Koth Melan]] Koth Melan was a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] of the prominent [[Clan Alya]] hailing from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. His [[Koth Melan's father|father]] was a [[Teacher/Legends|teacher]], who disagreed with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] ideology and attempted to educate his students about the Empire's nature.''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' In [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] states that Melan's [[Parent/Legends|father]] was [[Death/Legends|executed]] twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before its events. The novel is dated to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''; therefore, the death of Melan's father can be dated to [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]. Melan's [[Parent/Legends|father]] was arrested by the Empire, and [[Death/Legends|executed]] on charges of [[Spy/Legends|espionage]]. The execution was a blow to Melan's honor, and caused him much disgrace. He stopped using''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' the honorific suffix "y'lya" as part of his name, vowing to only honor his clan name once he had helped to cause the downfall of the Empire, and in turn avenge his father's death. Melan joined the [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]]; officially, he was an [[assistant consul general]] for the [[Bothan Trade Mission]], and was responsible for overseeing Bothan commercial interests throughout the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] region. His position was an intricately crafted cover which allowed him to travel extensively without arousing suspicion, passing information from operatives and [[op-front]]s to the relevant interested parties and frequently reporting to his superiors on Bothawui and the colony world [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]]. While his cover was unbroken, Melan made enemies, and always traveled with a cadre of combat trained [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] skilled in counter-intelligence and surveillance operations who posed as minor officials and [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrats]]. At some point, he became the head of the entire Spynet.''[[Hero's Guide]]'' Melan rarely engaged directly in operations, preferring the role of [[Spymaster/Legends|spymaster]]. He refused to meet with anyone whom he did not wish to see first, and maintained a series of secure safehouses including one which was known only to Melan and his entourage. When Melan did venture into the field, it was a sign that the information he was collecting was very valuable and important. His [[Koth Melan's chief of security|chief of security]] was often employed as a double, and reportedly performed a credible imitation of the spymaster. ===Observing the Alliance=== {{Quote|There are agents of the Empire here on Corellia. We must be careful. In fact, there is one watching us.|Koth Melan|{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}}}} [[File:Bothan_Traitors.jpg|thumb|right|Koth Melan and Dash Rendar]] During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Melan began to take a keen interest in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] movements, despite the Bothan Spynet's supposed lack of allegiance to any particular government or organization. When Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]], with the help of his [[Azzameen family|family]], stealthily planted a listening device at [[Comm Center]] to monitor communications between the Empire and the Azzameen's main commercial rivals, the [[Viraxo]], Melan was highly impressed. The spymaster sent Azzameen an [[e-mail]], applauding his abilities, labeling them "Bothan," and awarded him the [[Bothan Friendship Award]], as well as honorary membership of the Bothan Spynet.''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' At some point, Melan met with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]] near the [[Agrilat Region|Agrilat]] [[Agrilat Swamp Circuit|Swamp Circuit]] on the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. He handed Rendar a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] with information vital for the Alliance, but noticed that the smuggler was being trailed by an Imperial operative. The operative, in league with the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]], was attempting to expose Rendar as a Rebel traitor who was supplying arms and equipment to the Alliance. During the ensuing [[Lightfight|battle]], Melan was wounded and ordered Rendar to flee with the information. The [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] stopped for a moment, but then made his escape, leaving Melan to surrender to the Imperial. In a few [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], clad in [[Binders/Legends|binders]], he was transported to [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jeffren Brek]] of the Imperial Security Bureau in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Bela Vistal/Legends|Bela Vistal]]. The datapad, however, found its way into the hands of Alliance member [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] when Rendar handed it over to the young [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}} ===Investigating Imperial projects=== {{Quote|From what little else we've learned of it, this project does not bode well for the Alliance.|Koth Melan|}} Some time later, Melan had regained his freedom, and returned to Bothawui. He lived at [[Safehouse Besh]] on the planet, and was accompanied by several security agents when he traveled. On one occasion, while returning home, his security escort found a foreign object on the hatch leading to Safehouse Besh. Concerned that it was an explosive, two of the agents, [[SecOps Four]] and [[SecOps Seven]], investigated while [[SecOps Five]] remained with Melan. They found that the attachment was merely a [[flexiplast]] flyer for [[Krazy Khzam's Repulsor Lot]], although they did test it for contact [[Poison/Legends|poisons]], electronic devices and sharp edges. With no danger apparent, Melan entered his home where he instructed SecOps Seven to pour him some [[Gruvian Tovash]].{{WEGCite|story=Safehouse Besh (short story)|book=Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook}} [[File:Koth_Melan_hologram.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Koth Melan's message droid is intercepted by Luke Skywalker and Dash Rendar.]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], Melan's operatives bribed a high-ranking [[Unidentified Imperial officer (bribe)|Imperial officer]] and learned that the Empire had began work on a [[Death Star II/Legends|highly-secret construction project]], into which vast amounts of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]], materiel, and personnel were being diverted. Melan attempted to infiltrate a [[Slicer droid/Legends|slicer droid]] into the [[main computer complex]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], but the effort met with failure. However, the failure brought to light the fact that information pertaining to the project was being stored on special [[Computer/Legends|computers]] with no remote access. To obtain the data, operatives would have to physically get their hands on the computer. Melan's operatives learned that one of the computers containing data relevant to the project was being transferred from Coruscant to Bothawui. The Empire planned to use the [[MB-C1 medium transport|Mobquet medium transport]] ''[[Suprosa]]'' to make the transfer, disguising it as a fertilizer barge. By utilizing a low-key craft instead of a massive convoy, Melan believed that they hoped to avoid Alliance attention. While Melan's operatives worked to obtain the vessel's flight plan, he attempted to contact [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the Alliance, requesting that she meet him in the [[Intergalactic Trade Mission]] on Bothawui. Initially believing her to be on Tatooine, he dispatched an [[Seeker AS-M12 message droid|AS-M12-series messenger droid]] to the planet. However, while his [[Courier droid/Legends|message droid]] was in transit, he learned that Organa was in fact on the planet [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]], and that Luke Skywalker and Dash Rendar were the likely ones to receive the message. As expected, Skywalker and his bodyguard arrived on Bothawui, seeking Melan. The Bothan allowed the two into his presence, and explained the situation to the two [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Skywalker's presence was a stroke of luck; Melan had enlisted the Bothan [[BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-S3 Y-wing starfighter]] pilots of [[Blue Squadron (Bothan)|Blue Squadron]] to help capture the ''Suprosa'', but they were inexperienced and would benefit from the experience of a veteran in command. Skywalker agreed, and Melan took the two to a hidden [[mountain base]] to meet with the squadron. Skywalker led the group in training exercises for several [[Standard day/Legends|days]], preparing them to meet the ''Suprosa'' in the [[Both system]]. However, the squadron was one pilot short, and Ace Azzameen, the Alliance pilot from the ''Liberty'' honored by Melan earlier, was chosen to fill the vacant slot by Skywalker himself based on the ''Liberty''{{'s}} pilot credits listings.[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] ===Raid on the ''Suprosa''=== {{Dialogue|Luke Skywalker|If this information is right, how long before we need to launch and get ready?|Koth Melan|Your squad of volunteers has already been put on alert. We'll need to be in position in less than three standard hours to make the rendezvous.|Skywalker|'We'?|Melan|I'll be going along. If Dash Rendar has room on his ship?|Dash Rendar|No problem. Can you cook? Might want to grab a bite while we collect this freighter.|attr=Luke Skywalker, Koth Melan, and Dash Rendar|src=}} One of Melan's operatives obtained the ''Suprosa''{{'s}} flight plan from a contact within [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], a large criminal syndicate. The source of the information did not bother the Bothan despite both Skywalker and Rendar's concerns as to its source. Melan believed that Black Sun were backing the Alliance to win the war, and noted that war usually made strange bedfellows. With Melan aboard Dash Rendar's [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]] [[Outrider/Legends|''Outrider'']], Skywalker led Blue Squadron to one of Bothawui's [[Moon/Legends|moons]] to await the arrival of the ''Suprosa''. As the freighter decanted from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], Skywalker ordered the ''Suprosa'' to surrender, which was met with the claim that the ship was hauling fertilizer. Eventually, the ''Suprosa'' attempted to flee, and Skywalker, Rendar, and Blue Squadron engaged the craft, attempting to slow its flight. The freighter unveiled concealed weapons, and [[Raid on the Suprosa|battle commenced]]; two Bothan Y-wings were destroyed by defensive fire, while four craft were eliminated by a single [[diamond boron missile]] that Rendar was unable to destroy before it exploded in their midst. Eventually, the ''Suprosa'' was disabled, and Rendar boarded first, clearing the Imperial presence[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]] before Melan and a group of his operatives retrieved the computer. Skywalker rendezvoused with them aboard the ''Outrider'', and Melan explained that he would be unable to access the data as it was undoubtedly encrypted and rigged with a self-destruct mechanism. His best [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] team was on Kothlis, a Bothan colony a few [[Light-year/Legends|light-years]] distant. The group left the Both system for Kothlis, but ''en route'' they were pulled out of hyperspace by the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] [[Claw (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Claw'']], escorted by the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Hunter (Victory I-class)|''Hunter'']]. While Skywalker and Rendar engaged the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] deployed by the ''Hunter'', Azzameen led the remnants of Blue Squadron against the ''Claw'', collapsing its [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and forcing it to withdraw. With the [[Interdiction field/Legends|interdiction field]] down, the group was free to continue to Kothlis where Rendar dropped Melan and his team off on the [[Kothlis II]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] before departing for Rodia to update Princess Organa on the situation. Melan met with Skywalker on the surface of Kothlis, escorting him to a safehouse on the outskirts of the city where his team were working on decrypting the computer. The safe house, disguised as a row of storage units and dilapidated offices, was a highly modern installation filled with the latest computer technology. As Melan and Skywalker watched, one of the Bothan slicers found some interesting information in Sector [[Tarp-Hard-Xenon]]. Before Melan could find out what the excitement was about, the door to the installation was blown inwards, followed by individuals firing [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. ===Death and legacy=== {{Quote|G-G-Go! Leave me, get out! […] Idiot! L-L-Leave! […] Luke, thanks. I—|Koth Melan's dying words|}} [[File:Kothmelan.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Melan dies on Kothlis.]] A team of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] led by the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Skahtul]] and the [[Kajain'sa'Nikto/Legends|Kajain'sa'Nikto]] [[Calliose]] invaded the safehouse, intent on claiming a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Skywalker's head. During the confusion, several of the Bothan slicers were gunned down, while Melan, armed with a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], and Skywalker attempted to hold off the attack. Believing that the attackers were after the computer, he urged Skywalker to retreat further into the complex. One of the slicers pulled the computer from the slicing equipment, and Melan ordered him to run for it while they covered him. The slicer escaped through an unmarked emergency exit just as Melan's blaster ran out of shots. Ordering Skywalker to run, the Jedi was about to when Melan was hit by a blaster bolt. Despite the Bothan's demands to leave him, Skywalker stayed and defended the spymaster. As the wounded Melan lay on the ground, he caught the attention of one of his bodyguards, SecOps Four. SecOps Four, who had been charged with Melan's safety, had failed his duty. However, Melan looked at him not in disappointment, but in hope. SecOps Four understood, and grabbed a case containing stolen Imperial data and fled down the escape corridor.{{WEGCite|book=Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook|story=Hand-Off}} Melan died shortly after, and Skywalker was captured by the bounty hunters. Skywalker eventually managed to escape from the bounty hunters, and the information ended up in the hands of the Alliance by way of [[Borsk Fey'lya]]'s group of Bothan spies, despite an [[Battle of Kothlis|attempt]] by the Empire to retrieve the information, and the Alliance learned the Empire was constructing a second [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]].{{Film|VI}} However, the attack at Kothlis was part of an elaborate ruse perpetrated by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who wanted the Alliance to learn the location of the construction site of the second Death Star so that he could draw them into a [[Battle of Endor/Legends|trap]]. Melan's funeral was held on Bothawui; pilots from the ''Liberty'' were granted permission to attend but only if they were already on leave at the time. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|There's not much to see, I'm afraid. Unless you're an expert in this, the information looks to be pretty much a jumble of numbers and letters.''"
"''What do they mean?''"
"''Got me. I'm a spymaster. What I know about programming you could inscribe on a microdiode lead with a dull sword.|Koth Melan and Luke Skywalker|}} [[File:KothMelan-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Melan defends his agents from Barabel attack.]] Koth Melan was a short [[Color/Legends|brown]]-furred Bothan with black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. A proud individual, he had the utmost faith in the abilities of his Spynet and liked to keep abreast of the movements of important political figures, usually knowing where any one of them was at any given time. Though he lacked technical expertise, he made sure to employ a broad range of [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] with the requisite technical skills. Melan had no qualms with dealing with criminal organizations such as Black Sun, as long as it was all in the pursuit of defeating the Empire. Melan had a strict adherence to Bothan culture, subjecting himself to the harsh laws of honor. He suffered the consequences of his father's death by styling his name in a fashion foreign to Bothans. Though he stood to gain considerably more by remaining a neutral party in the Galactic Civil War, he hoped his siding with the Alliance would help him in the pursuit of honor. In [[Bothese]], Melan's given name meant "philosophical." His family name translated as "moon," or "night."''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'' An adept spymaster, Melan was well informed and knowledgeable about other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], their cultures and languages, and planetary [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. As the head of a Spynet op-front, and the official assistant consul general of the Bothan Trade Mission, Melan was well versed in bureaucratic protocol for both organizations, as well as Clan Alya itself. The spymaster also knew about general law enforcement procedures, business and the value of items. As part of his duties involved dealing with criminals or fugitives from the law, Melan had contacts in the underworld and was trained in survival techniques. In his role as a spy, Melan was sometimes required to intimidate, bargain with, persuade or con other individuals into revealing their secrets, skills which he displayed a degree of skill in. He was a shrewd investigator, could be stealthy when the need arose, and was an adept [[Thief/Legends|pick pocket]] and [[forger]]. Although he admitted that computer programming and slicing was beyond knowledge range, Melan was trained in general computer programming and skills, as well as droid programming. He could also competently operate communications, security, and sensor systems, and drive [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] vehicles. He was trained in the use of a blaster, and carried a long-barreled hold-out pistol affixed to his waist and right leg. Melan could defend himself in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]], and apply [[Medic/Legends|first aid]] if needed. He generally carried a datapad and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Melan, do you think you'll be able to access the program?''"
"''No, it'll be encrypted and protected by a self-destruct device. Our best team is on Kothlis, a colony not too far from here. We'll transport it there, and see what we've got.''"
"''Are we apt to run into any more Imperials along the way?''"
"''Who can say? It is possible…|[[Bob Bergen]] as Luke Skywalker and Denny Delk as Koth Melan|audio=KothMelan.flac|}} [[File:WerewolvesOfBothawui-AOTBH.png|thumb|right|A model wearing a Lon Chaney mask was used to pose as Melan for the ''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card series]] Koth Melan first appeared in the [[1996]] [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]], written by [[Steve Perry]]. The novel was a part of the ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' multimedia project, and Melan was featured in other media adaptations of the ''Shadows of the Empire'' storyline.[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] The same year, Melan's background was detailed in [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]''. Melan would later appear in the [[1999]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', which crosses over with the events from ''Shadows of the Empire''. In ''X-Wing Alliance'', he is voiced by [[Denny Delk]], though Delk was simply credited as "Bothan Officer." Melan was depicted on several cards in [[Topps]]' [[1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card series]]. The artists who illustrated the cards, [[Greg Hildebrandt|Greg]] and [[Tim Hildebrandt]], had a live model pose for Melan's action shots. His alien look was based on a mask of [[Wikipedia:Lon Chaney, Jr.|Lon Chaney, Jr.'s]] "[[Wikipedia:The Wolf Man (1941 film)|Wolf Man]]" character, and a [[:File:Koth Melan.jpg|sketch]] by [[Michael Butkus]] provided by Lucasfilm''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]'' which was also used in the concurrent ''Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook''. In [[2003]], the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 61|61st issue]] of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' mentioned Melan in its entry on messenger droids,{{FactFile|61|MES 1-2|Messenger Droids}} using an image that was originally of a generic Bothan spy on a card in [[Decipher]]'s [[1998]] ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' release for the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] to represent Melan. This effectively [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] Melan as appearing in the 1998 card expansion.{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Bothan Spy}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Koth Melan}} *{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}} *''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (abridged audiobook)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' abridged audiobook]] *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]] {{Hologram}} Bothans & Dresselians}} *{{CCG|set=Special Edition Limited|cardname=Bothan Spy}} {{ID|Bothan Spy}} {{Ret}} *{{FactFile|25|REN 1-6|Dash Rendar}} *{{FactFile|61|MES 1-2|Messenger Droids}} *''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'' *''[[Hero's Guide]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{SWGTCG|set=Agents of Deception|cardname=Koth Melan}} {{CardGameSet|set={{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|parent=1}}|cards= *{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Bothan Traitors}} *{{SWGTCG|set=The Shadow Syndicate|cardname=Koth Melan|swglink=Koth Melan (Avatar) (card).JPG}} }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Koth Melan |nl=Koth Melan |pt=Legends:Koth Melan }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Melan, Koth}} [[Category:Agents of the Rebel Alliance]] [[Category:Bothans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Spies]]
